mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal broad-based economic strength and rising aggregate demand. This translates directly into higher expected corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC labor market recovery is a key focus for investors.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient (IC) is negative over any 3-year rolling period, or the top quintile of signals fails to outperform the bottom quintile.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below a critical threshold, it signals a severe contraction in a key cyclical sector with strong forward and backward linkages. This shock negatively impacts consumer wealth, durable goods spending, and financial sector stability, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average falls below 1,200 thousands of units.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts positive returns over the subsequent 60-day period.

mechanism_narrative: A stagflationary environment, characterized by rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) alongside slowing or contracting industrial production (INDPRO), creates significant uncertainty. This combination squeezes corporate margins and forces the central bank into a difficult trade-off, increasing risk premia and driving equity prices down.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPIAUCSL change > 3.5% while year-over-year INDPRO change < 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:1970s stagflationary period was negative for equities.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and INDPRO changes is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a walk-forward regression explaining 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary recovery, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates that supply chains are normalizing and firms are benefiting from high operating leverage. This leads to positive earnings surprises and drives strong positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current period (Jan 2011) is a recovery from the 2008-2009 recession.
failure_condition: The correlation between 3-month changes in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not positive and significant during NBER-defined recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below its estimated natural rate, it signals an overheating labor market. This leads to wage-push inflation, compresses corporate profit margins, and increases the probability of contractionary monetary policy, all of which are negative for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Late 1990s saw low unemployment precede a market downturn.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.5%, average 60-day forward returns are not statistically lower than when UNRATE is above 5.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: High-frequency changes in retail sales (RSAFS) provide a timely read on the health of the consumer, who accounts for the majority of US GDP. Positive surprises or strong upward momentum in retail sales lead to upward revisions in GDP and earnings forecasts, boosting equity prices over short horizons.
failure_condition: The hit rate of the signal (correctly predicting the 5-day return direction) is not statistically different from 50% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: An aggressive monetary policy stance, proxied by the Fed Funds rate rising significantly faster than real economic growth (GDPC1), signals the central bank is actively trying to slow the economy. This tightening of financial conditions reliably precedes economic downturns and bear markets.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS level > (2 * year-over-year % change in GDPC1) + 1.0
evidence_refs: fact:Yield curve inversions, a related concept, have historically preceded recessions.
failure_condition: In triggered periods, the S&P 500 experiences a positive 60-day return in more than 50% of instances over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a rapidly falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) while the central bank holds policy rates (FEDFUNDS) steady indicates that monetary policy is falling 'behind the curve'. Markets anticipate future, more aggressive rate hikes to catch up, increasing the discount rate on future earnings and causing negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 12-month change in UNRATE < -1.5pp AND 3-month change in FEDFUNDS < 0.1pp
failure_condition: A strategy going long equities when the trigger is active does not underperform a buy-and-hold benchmark on a risk-adjusted basis over a 10-year period.
